enforced2026_detail: Statewide ABAWD time limit enforcement resumed effective November 1, 2025 under OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, signed July 4, 2025), with the expanded age range (18-64) and narrowed exemptions. DHS news release treats the change as statewide. However, Hawaii holds special noncontiguous-state status: under OBBBA, Hawaii (and Alaska) may obtain geographic ABAWD waivers where an area's unemployment rate is at or above 1.5x the national average (rather than the standard >10% area threshold), and may temporarily exempt individuals for good-faith implementation effort, available through 2028. Because waiver coverage for qualifying high-unemployment areas remains possible under this special status, enforcement is best characterized as 'partial' rather than uniformly statewide. April 2026 USDA litigation guidance also reinstated benefits for some ABAWDs nationally.
formulaDetail: Flat federal baseline: at least 80 hours per month (an average of 20 hours per week), per Hawaii DHS ABAWD FAQ, the DHS/Governor news release, and HAR Title 17 Ch. 684.1 Subchapter 5 (which equates 'twenty hours a week averaged monthly' to 'eighty hours a month'). There is NO Hawaii allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula for self-initiated nonprofit volunteering. Volunteer service with a governmental or nonprofit organization counts directly toward the 80-hour requirement. (The allotment/min-wage formula seen in some search results is Pennsylvania's comparable-workfare program, not Hawaii.)
minimum_wage_note: Not applicable. Hawaii uses a flat 80-hour/month requirement; no allotment-over-minimum-wage formula governs nonprofit volunteer hours, so no minimum wage feeds the calculation. (For reference only, Hawaii's state minimum wage is $14.00/hr effective Jan 1, 2024, rising to $16.00/hr on Jan 1, 2026 — but this wage does NOT enter the ABAWD hours calculation.)
qualifying_org_definition: A governmental or nonprofit organization (e.g., a food pantry or homeless shelter). Volunteer work at a private for-profit company is not allowable.
qualifyingOrgShort: A governmental or nonprofit organization (e.g., food pantry or homeless shelter); for-profit volunteer work does not count.
verification_note: No Hawaii-specific named form for documenting community-service / volunteer hours was found in public sources. DHS publishes a general 'DHS 1266A Employment Verification Form' for paid employment, and households report via the Six Month Report Form (SMRF), but neither is a dedicated volunteer-hours certification. In operational practice, volunteer hours appear to be documented by a caseworker-verified written statement or letter from the nonprofit (standard across states without a named form). HAR §17-684.1-58 defines 'unpaid work' as 'verified under standards established by the department' without naming an instrument.
submissionMechanism: Paper/in-person at a DHS processing center or by mail; periodic reporting via the Six Month Report Form (SMRF), with online account access at pais.dhs.hawaii.gov / pais-benefits.dhs.hawaii.gov. No dedicated online ABAWD-hours portal was found.
submissionDeadline: Changes in work/training hours must be reported within 10 days of the change (HAR §17-684.1, change-reporting rules); ABAWDs must report when work/training hours drop below 20 hours/week. Periodic verification occurs via the Six Month Report Form (SMRF) and at recertification. No separate stand-alone deadline for a volunteer-hours certificate is published.
submission_deadline: Within 10 days of a change in work/training hours; periodic SMRF and recertification otherwise. No dedicated volunteer-hours-form deadline published.
remote_note: Neither HAR nor DHS guidance addresses whether remote/virtual volunteering counts. Qualifying activity is defined only by the nature of the organization (governmental/nonprofit), not by location or modality. Silent.
accepts_outofstate_detail: DHS FAQ and HAR §17-684.1-58 describe qualifying orgs as 'governmental or nonprofit organization (e.g., food pantry, homeless shelter)' with no in-state/out-of-state distinction stated. Federal default (any qualifying nonprofit, 7 CFR §273.24) should apply absent a state restriction. Silent.
required_prestep_detail: No EDP, employment plan, MOU, county assignment, or workfare-site registration gates the volunteer lane. SNAP E&T is described by DHS as voluntary; the eligibility-worker E&T screening is a routine intake offer, not a gate. General SNAP work registration at application is a separate SNAP-wide obligation that does not gate ABAWD compliance via the volunteer lane.
geographic_waivers: Hawaii has special noncontiguous-state status under OBBBA (P.L. 119-21): geographic ABAWD waivers available through 2028 where an area's unemployment rate is at or above 1.5x the national average (vs. the standard >10% threshold for other states); good-faith-effort temporary exemptions also permitted. No specific county-level approved waivers were confirmed in public FNS/FNA listings as of June 2026, but partial waiver coverage of high-unemployment areas remains possible.
geographicWaivers: Special noncontiguous-state status under OBBBA through 2028: waivers available where area unemployment is >=1.5x the national average (vs. standard >10%), plus good-faith-effort temporary exemptions. No specific county-level approved waivers confirmed in public listings as of June 2026; partial coverage possible.
conflictsWithPrior: Prior file used field name 'hours_formula' only; this verified record adds a top-level 'classification' = 'federal_80' to match the required schema. No substantive change.

Prior file's submission notes referenced the 'Monthly Eligibility Report Form (MERF)'; Hawaii's current periodic report instrument is the 'Six Month Report Form (SMRF)' (per pais.dhs.hawaii.gov). Corrected here. (Both are periodic eligibility reports, not dedicated volunteer-hours forms.)
Prior file did not mention the general 'DHS 1266A Employment Verification Form'; noted here for completeness, though it is for paid employment, not volunteer hours.
Prior 'remote_stance' was 'unknown'; reclassified to 'silent' to match required enum (no source addresses remote volunteering; meaning is the same).
unresolved: No Hawaii-specific named form for certifying community-service/volunteer hours (verificationFormName remains 'unknown'). Operational practice appears to be a caseworker-verified nonprofit letter/statement.
Whether remote/virtual volunteering counts is not addressed by any HI source (silent).
Whether an out-of-state 501(c)(3) may certify hours is not addressed by any HI source (silent).
Exact deadline/cadence for submitting a stand-alone volunteer-hours certificate (as distinct from the 10-day change report and the SMRF/recertification cycle) is not published.
Whether/which specific Hawaii areas currently hold OBBBA noncontiguous-state ABAWD waivers in FY2026 was not confirmable in public FNS/FNA county listings.
notes: VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22): Re-verified classification against live primary sources. Hawaii is FEDERAL_80 — a flat 80 hours/month (avg 20 hrs/week) requirement with NO allotment-over-minimum-wage formula for self-initiated nonprofit volunteering. Confirmed verbatim by (1) Hawaii DHS ABAWD FAQ ('at least 80 hours in a month (an average of 20 hours per week)'; 'Volunteer activity with a governmental or nonprofit organization (e.g., a food pantry or a homeless shelter, etc.)' counts; for-profit volunteering not allowable); (2) DHS/Governor news release ('complete an average of 80 hours per month (20 hours per week) in qualifying work or training activity'; 'volunteer service with a government or nonprofit organization'); (3) HAR Title 17 Ch. 684.1 Subchapter 5. Enforcement resumed Nov 1, 2025 under OBBBA with age range 18-64. The allotment/min-wage formula that surfaced in searches belongs to Pennsylvania's comparable-workfare program, NOT Hawaii — explicitly ruled out for HI. General nonprofit volunteering is a clean standalone lane that any recipient can self-initiate at any qualifying nonprofit with no provider registration, EDP, or E&T enrollment (SNAP E&T is voluntary per DHS). minimumWageUsd set to 0 because no wage feeds the HI calculation (Hawaii's actual state min wage is $14.00 now / $16.00 from Jan 1 2026, but it is NOT used in the ABAWD hours math). Remaining genuine unknowns: no named volunteer-hours form; remote and out-of-state-nonprofit stances are silent in all HI sources. Confidence HIGH: primary DHS sources directly confirm classification + 80 hours + qualifying nonprofit lane; only the verification form name and a few peripheral fields remain unresolved (operational details that do not affect classification).
